name: Zerodha Trade Pair Analyzer
scenario: Zerodha's reconciliation team needs to find pairs and triplets in 1M daily trade records matching specific profit/loss targets. The current O(n²) solution takes 14 hours. Your two-pointer solutions should complete in under 10 seconds.

core.description: twoSumSorted(int[] sorted, int target): two-pointer O(n), return int[] {i,j} or null. Test: [2,7,11,15] target=9 → [0,1]. Test: [1,2,3,4] target=10 → null.
threeSum(int[] nums): all unique triplets summing to 0. Sort + fix + TP. Skip all duplicates. Test: [-1,0,1,2,-1,-4] → [[-1,-1,2],[-1,0,1]]. Test: [0,0,0] → [[0,0,0]].
containerMaxWater(int[] height): O(n) two pointers. Move shorter side. Test: [1,8,6,2,5,4,8,3,7] → 49. Test: [1,1] → 1.
removeDuplicatesSorted(int[] sorted): in-place, return k. slow/fast pointers. Test: [0,0,1,1,1,2,2,3,3,4] → k=5, array starts [0,1,2,3,4].
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): twoSum correct, threeSum finds all triplets, maxWater=49 for [1,8,6,2,5,4,8,3,7], removeDups k=5. All pass.
README: explains WHY moving the shorter pointer in containerMaxWater is optimal (greedy proof in 2 sentences).

bonus.description: trappingRainWater(int[] height): O(n) two pointer, O(1) space. Test: [0,1,0,2,1,0,1,3,2,1,2,1] → 6. Test: [4,2,0,3,2,5] → 9.
sortedSquares(int[] nums): O(n) two pointers fill from back. Test: [-4,-1,0,3,10] → [0,1,9,16,100].
fourSum(int[] nums, int target): O(n³). Two outer loops + TP. Test: [1,0,-1,0,-2,2] target=0 → [[-2,-1,1,2],[-2,0,0,2],[-1,0,0,1]].

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Two pointers require SORTED array: unsorted → sort first (O(n log n) total). Pair sum: lo++ when sum too small (both values increase), hi-- when sum too large (both values decrease). Container water: move the SHORTER pointer — if you move the taller one, min(height) can only decrease while width always decreases. Three Sum duplicates: skip nums[i]==nums[i-1] for outer, nums[lo]==nums[lo+1] and nums[hi]==nums[hi-1] after finding triplet. Remove duplicates slow/fast: fast always advances; slow advances only when nums[fast] != nums[fast-1].
